item.title: FirstFT: Oil supply shock intensifies as final Hormuz tankers reach refineries
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Trump feuds with the pope, and Meta builds AI version of Mark Zuckerberg

item.title: Starmer accused of ‘corrosive complacency’ on UK defence by former Nato chief
item.description: Lord George Robertson, author of British military review, says premier is not willing to make the necessary investment

item.title: SEC boss tells retail investors to ‘stay out of the kitchen’ on private credit
item.description: Paul Atkins tells audience at spring IMF meetings that $1.8tn lending sector ‘is not a systemic risk’

item.title: Bain & Co vulnerability exposed by hacker a month after McKinsey
item.description: CodeWall says it gained access to consultant’s Pyxis platform using a username and password from public web code

item.title: Oil supply crunch intensifies as last Hormuz tankers reach refineries
item.description: Final prewar cargoes to leave Gulf set to unload in coming days, escalating a global rush to secure barrels

item.title: Trump deletes ‘Jesus’ post after backlash from allies
item.description: US president circulated image of himself as Christ-like figure shortly after lashing out at Pope Leo

item.title: Former Lafarge chief sentenced to six years in jail for financing terrorism
item.description: Paris court finds cement maker guilty of paying jihadis to keep operations running in Syria after civil war broke out

item.title: Farage and Kwarteng’s bitcoin treasury company has just one non-executive director. Who is she?
item.description: The mysterious oil tycoon in charge of board oversight at Stack BTC

item.title: Goldman Sachs retains its knack for spinning anxiety into gold
item.description: AI-inspired stock routs, the war in Iran and private credit were no hindrance to the Wall Street bank

item.title: LVMH sales hit as Middle East conflict delays luxury recovery
item.description: US and Israel’s war on Iran dented sales growth at world’s biggest luxury group

item.title: Robot-Proof — can the next generation keep a step ahead of the machines?
item.description: Vivienne Ming argues for a change in how we prepare the young for a near-future dominated and ‘deprofessionalised’ by AI

item.title: Hungary’s election could support Europe’s revival trade
item.description: In the long run, the result may be felt far beyond Budapest

item.title: US judge dismisses Trump lawsuit against Wall Street Journal
item.description: Ruling finds newspaper did not act with malice in report over letter in birthday book for Jeffrey Epstein

item.title: AI chatbots misdiagnose in over 80% of early medical cases, study finds
item.description: Top models including OpenAI and DeepSeek make judgments too quickly when patient data is incomplete

item.title: Opec production falls more than a quarter as Iran war hits oil exports
item.description: Output dropped by biggest volume on record in March with closure of Strait of Hormuz hitting producers around the Gulf

item.title: Oil prices soar above $100 a barrel
item.description: Crude had stabilised on optimism that a deal could be reached to reopen the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: New EU competition official to hold line on Big Tech despite Trump pressure
item.description: Anthony Whelan, a former adviser on tech, vows to pursue cases irrespective of ‘noise’ around them

item.title: Bank of England wins US approval for new way to rescue failed lenders
item.description: Regulators gain more flexibility if ‘bail-in’ process is required in response to a crisis

item.title: Battery giant CATL is no longer just a play on electric vehicles
item.description: The Chinese group’s expansion into non-automotive areas will benefit from policy-driven demand to offset consumer cycles

item.title: The AI build-out is powering global goods trade
item.description: Data centre boom is helping to mask the impact of Trump tariffs on US and world economy

item.title: Goldman reports best quarter in 5 years even as fixed-income traders disappoint
item.description: Equities traders powered bank’s earnings, while fixed-income, currencies and commodities business fell well short

item.title: Why time is on Iran’s side
item.description: A global energy crisis is only just beginning. Political turmoil will follow

item.title: Perella Weinberg to buy London advisory boutique Gleacher Shacklock
item.description: Acquisition is latest move by US investment banks to expand in Europe

item.title: FirstFT: Oil price surges ahead of Strait of Hormuz blockade
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Viktor Orbán suffers crushing defeat and Meta develops AI version of Mark Zuckerberg

item.title: Private credit has calmed the credit cycle
item.description: We’d miss it if it went

item.title: Viktor Orbán ousted after 16 years in landslide defeat for anti-EU torchbearer
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Brussels’ energy crisis remedies and Green Deal architect returns

item.title: Bill Ackman plays to the market
item.description: Plus, Nest’s private credit mandate, US small caps jump and Rothko in Florence

item.title: How the Federal Reserve could shrink its balance sheet
item.description: Suggestions for how the US central bank can lower its influence in financial markets are coming thick and fast

item.title: Five UK water companies spent more than £32mn on appeal to raise bills
item.description: Utilities argued for additional increases to attract investors and avoid breaching licence conditions

item.title: Weight-loss drugs and Mars bars: Novo Nordisk’s comeback bid
item.description: The maker of Wegovy and Ozempic wants to learn lessons from consumer groups to crack the US market
